lesson: Construct aware datetimes with zoneinfo.ZoneInfo(tzinfo=...) and convert between zones with astimezone(), never by hand-shifting hours.
Use strptime with an explicit format string containing %z to parse offsets like '-0500' directly into an aware datetime, and strftime with the same format to round-trip.
Remember timedelta normalizes to 0<=seconds<86400 and 0<=microseconds<1e6 with days absorbing sign, so a negative timedelta like -1 hour shows as days=-1, seconds=82800, not seconds=-3600.
Measure elapsed durations with time.monotonic(), not time.time(), because wall-clock reads can jump backward from NTP/manual corrections and produce negative or nonsensical diffs.
task: monotonic clock vs wall clock for duration measurement

lesson: Adding timedelta to a zoneinfo-aware datetime does wall-clock arithmetic on naive fields, not real-elapsed-time; convert to UTC, add there, then astimezone() back across a DST transition.
task: DST-safe timedelta arithmetic via UTC round-trip

lesson: datetime equality across differing tzinfo compares by absolute instant automatically, but comparing a naive datetime against an aware one raises TypeError -- normalize first with astimezone().
During a DST fall-back transition, disambiguate a repeated local hour with fold=0/1 (first/second occurrence) -- same naive fields, but utcoffset() and timestamp() differ by an hour.
task: PEP 495 fold attribute for ambiguous fall-back local times

lesson: Prefer time.monotonic_ns() integer accumulation over summing many time.monotonic() float diffs -- sub-microsecond float additions compound binary rounding error over many iterations; integer nanosecond sums stay exact.
task: float rounding drift vs integer-nanosecond accumulation in duration totals
